meta.title: AI Software Development Cost for Custom AI Systems
meta.description: Estimate ai software development cost and ship custom LLM apps with RAG, agents, guardrails, evals, and compliance controls.

hero.h1: ai software development cost for custom AI software
hero.subtitle: Build production LLM apps with predictable token spend, evaluation, and compliance-ready guardrails.

pain.title: You need predictable outcomes, not surprises
bullets: Have you ever built an LLM prototype that worked in demos, then failed on real company data?
Have you ever shipped a chatbot, then watched token cost climb with no way to trace what changed?
Have you ever worried about PII, retention, or audit needs after the app was already in production?

benefits.title: Cost you can forecast
benefits.desc: We model token cost, latency, and throughput early, then design prompts, caching, and retrieval to keep spend stable.

benefits.title: Answers you can trust
benefits.desc: RAG with embeddings, grounded citations, and quality evals reduces hallucinations and makes regressions visible before release.

benefits.title: Controls for real environments
benefits.desc: Guardrails, prompt observability, and AI compliance practices help you meet security expectations and operate with confidence.

scope.title: What you get with custom AI software
bullets: LLM selection: OpenAI, Anthropic, or Llama based on quality, privacy, and budget
RAG with embeddings: chunking, retrieval strategy, reranking, and relevance evaluation
Vector database: pgvector for Postgres-native needs or Pinecone for managed scale
Agents with tool use: call internal APIs, search docs, write tickets, and update systems with permissions
Fine-tuning when it pays off: task fit analysis, datasets, and safe rollout plans
Guardrails: policy rules, PII detection/redaction, prompt injection defenses, and content filters
Prompt observability: traces, versioning, feedback capture, and error taxonomy for fast debugging
Token cost controls: rate limits, caching, batching, and per-feature budget alerts
AI compliance support: data handling, retention, access controls, audit logs, and vendor review inputs

useCases.title: Knowledge base assistant
useCases.desc: Search and answer from SOPs, policies, and docs using RAG with citations and relevance checks.

useCases.title: Customer support copilot
useCases.desc: Draft responses, summarize tickets, and pull account context with tool use while respecting data permissions.

useCases.title: Document intake automation
useCases.desc: Extract fields, classify, and route PDFs and emails with eval-backed accuracy targets and human review loops.

useCases.title: Sales and enablement helper
useCases.desc: Generate tailored outreach and proposals grounded in your product knowledge and approved messaging.

useCases.title: Internal workflow agent
useCases.desc: Automate repetitive ops tasks by chaining tools safely: create tasks, update records, and escalate exceptions.

options.name: OpenAI
options.note: Strong general capability for chat, extraction, and tool use with flexible model choices.

options.name: Anthropic
options.note: Great for long-context reasoning and careful instruction following in enterprise workflows.

options.name: Llama
options.note: Self-hosted or private deployments when you need tighter control over data and infrastructure.

options.name: pgvector
options.note: Keep vector search inside Postgres for simpler ops and consistent access control.

options.name: Pinecone
options.note: Managed vector DB for high-scale retrieval workloads and operational simplicity.

options.name: Prompt observability
options.note: Tracing, prompt/version diffing, and cost analytics so you can debug and control spend.

steps.title: Scope and estimate
steps.desc: Define success metrics, risks, and a cost model for tokens, retrieval, and hosting.

steps.title: Build and evaluate
steps.desc: Implement RAG, agents, and guardrails, then run quality evals and safety checks before launch.

steps.title: Deploy and operate
steps.desc: Add observability, alerts, and compliance controls, then iterate with measured improvements.

faq.q: What drives ai software development cost the most?
faq.a: Scope, data readiness, and the target reliability level. Adding RAG, evals, and observability typically costs more upfront, but prevents expensive production issues and token waste.

faq.q: Do you recommend fine-tuning or RAG first?
faq.a: Usually RAG first, because embeddings plus retrieval can ground answers on your data with faster iteration. Fine-tuning can help for consistent formats or style, but we only use it when evaluation shows a clear win.

faq.q: How do you control token cost in production?
faq.a: We design for budget: prompt minimization, caching, batching, rate limits, and retrieval that reduces unnecessary context. We also ship dashboards and alerts so you can track cost by feature and version.

faq.q: When should I use pgvector vs Pinecone?
faq.a: pgvector fits teams that want Postgres-native operations and unified access control. Pinecone fits managed scale and high-throughput retrieval where you want to offload vector operations to a specialized service.

faq.q: How do you handle AI compliance and sensitive data?
faq.a: We map data flows, apply least-privilege access, and add audit logs and retention controls. Guardrails can include PII redaction, policy checks, and safe tool-use boundaries so the system behaves predictably.

ctaFinal.title: Get a clear estimate before you build
ctaFinal.desc: Share your use case and data constraints, and we will map scope, risks, and a plan to manage quality and token cost.
